_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Train and evaluate machine learning models.
reference_document_element_description: Design, train, evaluate, and document machine learning models, including feature engineering decisions, algorithm selection rationale, and evaluation results against baseline.
comments: DCWF task equal WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-001 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RM-K-002 (RM, cosine: 0.579), AI-T-005 (AI, cosine: 0.514).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.8/10 (raw 0.685). Shared vocabulary: [evaluate, learning, machine, models, train].

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.916 (P=0.957, R=0.878). P exceeds R by 0.080 — the DCWF element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 20 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Train and evaluate machine learning models." and "Design, train, evaluate, and document machine learning models, including feature" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.659 ≈ STS 0.685 (gap 0.026). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [evaluate, learning, machine, models, train] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 31.6%. Shared tokens: [evaluate, learning, machine, models, train]. DCWF-only: []. WIDAI-only: [against, algorithm, baseline, decisions, design, document].
